layoutRegions.name: remediation-summary
layoutRegions.purpose: At-a-glance counts for critical removals, missed SLA windows, dependency blockers, and ready cleanup batches.

layoutRegions.name: candidate-board
layoutRegions.purpose: Keyboard-focusable rows for risk-ranked access remediation candidates.

layoutRegions.name: cleanup-batches
layoutRegions.purpose: Panels for owner assignments, reviewer notes, dependency blockers, and rollback-safe cleanup batches.

components.name: CandidateRow
components.purpose: Focusable remediation candidate row with risk, owner, SLA, blocker, and cleanup batch context.
interactions: hover and focus-visible states expose row selection
priority state is shown with semantic danger, warning, info, or success

components.name: BatchCard
components.purpose: Cleanup batch card that keeps removals, rollback command, and window visible.
interactions: tone label stays visible without overlapping text
card sizing remains stable across responsive breakpoints

components.name: GuardrailList
components.purpose: Ordered cleanup checklist for rollback-safe remediation batches.
interactions: primary cleanup action has visible hover and focus-visible states
guardrail numbers are stable and token-colored

acceptanceChecks: Generated app imports src/omarchy-theme.css before app styles.
Every visible color comes from a --omarchy-* CSS variable or color-mix using one.
Every button, row, input, and command item has a visible :focus-visible state.
Layout remains stable at 320px, 768px, and 1280px viewport widths.
Primary content is usable on first load without a marketing interstitial.
A .desktop launcher can be generated with omarchy-native app desktop.
An opt-in theme sync hook can be generated with omarchy-native app hook.
